channel.title: Energy & Environment
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Trump administration invokes emergency powers to restart oil operations off California coast
item.description: The Energy Department cited U.S. military dependence on foreign oil.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright on Friday issued an order that will have a significant impact on California's oil industry.

item.dc:creator: By Noah Baustin

item.title: ‘Oh, boy’: Oil industry frets over Trump's profit-minded Iran post
item.description: Trump’s social-media post is a potential public relations hurdle for an industry that has a complicated relationship with the president.

media:content.media:credit: Royal Thai Navy/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz are snarling oil-tanker traffic — and sending prices soaring.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre, James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre, James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo

item.title: Five reasons oil prices won't snap back from Iran war
item.description: Tanker backlogs, damaged energy infrastructure and threats in the Strait of Hormuz could keep gasoline prices elevated.

media:content.media:title: A man walks along the shore in the United Arab Emirates as oil tankers and cargo ships line up in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: White House considers waiving maritime commerce restrictions as fuel prices skyrocket
item.description: The move comes as the U.S. and Israel’s war in Iran has sent oil prices soaring, with a key choke point effectively blocked.

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: High gas prices are listed at Chevron gas station in Los Angeles on Monday as gasoline prices surge amid the ongoing war with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Iran war is the largest oil supply disruption in history, report finds
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday seemingly dismissed the market disruptions having a dramatic impact on the U.S. economy.

media:content.media:title: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway responsible for carrying roughly 20 percent of the world’s oil supply, has seen oil and product flows plunge from around 20 million barrels a day to "a trickle,” the International Energy Agency wrote.

item.dc:creator: By Gregory Svirnovskiy

item.title: Trump’s energy ‘tiger team’ struggles to find its roar with Iran
item.description: Trump's vaunted “energy dominance” team is in danger of fumbling the biggest energy crisis of his second term, critics say.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Claudine Hellmuth/POLITICO (source images via Getty)
media:content.media:title: Main.WrightBurgumOil.jpg

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales

item.title: US to release 172 million barrels of oil from strategic reserve to combat energy price hike
item.description: The Trump administration move is in conjunction with the International Energy Agency action and comes as shipments from the Persian Gulf remain constrained.

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Department of Energy via AP
media:content.media:title: A section of the Strategic Petroleum Reserve facility is shown in West Hackberry, Louisiana.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Carlos Anchondo

item.title: Countries confirm biggest emergency oil release in history
item.description: The International Energy Agency announced the release of 400 million barrels of oil in response to price shocks from the Iran war.

media:content.media:title: International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol gives a press conference in Brussels on March 6.

item.dc:creator: By James Fernyhough

item.title: A data center opened next door. Then came the high-pitched whine.
item.description: Getting data centers to generate their own electricity may ease one obstacle to public acceptance of data centers. But it creates new hurdles.

media:content.media:title: “We don’t want to be outside anymore,” said cybersecurity professional Lindsay Shaw, seen standing Monday next to a tech hub owned by Vantage Data Centers near her home in Sterling, Virginia. The structure behind her houses gas turbines that Shaw blames for noise pollution.

item.dc:creator: By Ariel Wittenberg

item.title: US official: ‘Not true’ Navy successfully escorted oil tanker through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright claimed in a now-deleted social media post that the Navy escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright (left) and Rep. Gabe Evans (R-Colo.) speak at the Fort St. Vrain Generating Station in Platteville, Colorado.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Paul McLeary

item.title: Oil climbs down from precipice as US crude reserve release ‘on the table’
item.description: The Trump administration hasn’t committed to joining allies in tapping oil stockpiles, and so far doesn’t consider oil supply to be a problem.

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Gasoline prices are expected to rise following a weeklong climb in oil prices amid the war with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Senator mocked ‘green energy crap.’ His house runs on it.
item.description: Montana Republican Tim Sheehy voted to scrap solar tax credits after installing panels and battery storage at his Bozeman home.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Tim Sheehy (R-Mont.) had solar panels installed on his home before he voted to end tax breaks for rooftop systems.

item.dc:creator: By Timothy Cama and Corbin Hiar
item.dc:contributor: By Timothy Cama and Corbin Hiar

item.title: How Democrats flipped 22 Georgia counties with a utility board race
item.description: The campaign for once-obscure regulatory positions could be a bellwether for midterm elections as skyrocketing electricity prices drive voters to the polls.

media:content.media:title: Homemade signs tout the candidacy of Democrat Peter Hubbard last year for Georgia's Public Service Commission.

item.dc:creator: By Arianna Skibell

item.title: Crude prices make record jump as Trump’s measures fail to calm markets
item.description: The Trump administration has yet to solve the fundamental issue of getting oil and gas tankers moving through the Strait of Hormuz again, analysts said.

media:content.media:title: A person fills their car with gas as fuel prices at a gas station are displayed behind them, March 4, 2026, in Baltimore.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Fed panel delays vote but ready to rumba over Trump’s ballroom
item.description: A long list of people critical of the president's plans for a massive White House ballroom signed up to speak before the National Capital Planning Commission.

media:content.media:title: Cranes are seen on the grounds of the White House on Feb. 25 as a federal panel considers plans for President Donald Trump's proposed $400 million ballroom.

item.dc:creator: By Michael Doyle

item.title: Susie Wiles sounds the alarm on gas prices
item.description: White House aides and Cabinet officials are coming under intense pressure to reverse the spike in energy prices caused by the start of the war in the Middle East.

media:content.media:title: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles is seen during a roundtable discussion where President Donald Trump announced a $12 billion aid plan for farmers in the Cabinet Room of the White House in Washington on Dec. 8, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: US mulls military support for vulnerable oil supplies in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump’s announcement is the first sign his administration is starting to take seriously the surge in fuel prices that began after the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks against Iran.

media:content.media:title: A speedboat operated by members of Iran's Revolutionary Guard approaches a British-flagged oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz in 2019.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and Jack Detsch

item.title: Rubio: Trump administration has plan to calm oil markets
item.description: The comments from the secretary of State come as Iran warns it will attack ships seeking to travel through the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

media:content.media:title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaks with reporters as he arrives to brief lawmakers on the U.S. strikes on Iran at the U.S. Capitol, March 2, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and Finya Swai
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and Finya Swai

item.title: 5 charts show China’s oil dilemma after US strikes
item.description: Disruption of Iranian oil supplies may make China more interested in turning to Russia.

media:content.media:title: Immigration inspection officers check an oil tanker carrying imported crude oil at Qingdao port in China's eastern Shandong province.

item.dc:creator: By Paroma Soni and Catherine Allen
item.dc:contributor: By Paroma Soni and Catherine Allen

item.title: One early risk of Trump's Iran strategy is already emerging
item.description: President Donald Trump’s decision to topple the Iranian regime is threatening to torch an oil market his administration has been at pains to keep cool since he returned to office.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump spoke in Corpus Christi, Texas, on Friday ahead of Saturday's military strikes.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and James Bikales

item.title: US attack in Iran poses bigger risk to energy market than Venezuela
item.description: A sustained U.S. military action against Tehran could set off a broader conflict in the Middle East and scramble global oil shipments.

media:content.media:title: A cargo ship is seen in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Feb. 25, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Solar power’s newest friends: MAGA influencers
item.description: The nation’s leading clean energy lobby aims to bolster solar power’s standing via conservative media partnerships, polling and Stephen Miller’s wife.

media:content.media:title: Stephen Miller and Katie Miller attend the 2025 Easter Egg Roll on the South Lawn of the White House on April 21, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Kelsey Brugger, Zack Colman and Pavan Acharya
item.dc:contributor: By Kelsey Brugger, Zack Colman and Pavan Acharya

item.title: Trump delayed a global carbon tax. Now he wants to finish the fight.
item.description: American officials are drafting a diplomatic cable that warns dozens of countries against adopting a climate fee on the shipping industry.

media:content.media:title: A container ship sails in front of the Manhattan skyline in New York in January.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt

item.title: Federal safety regulator warns his office can’t keep up with Trump’s Alaska oil push
item.description: The warning shows how the administration’s earlier DOGE-driven cuts to the federal workforce could cause problems for Trump’s other policy objectives.

media:content.media:title: The Trump administration is set to hold the first oil and gas lease sale in more than three years in Alaska's Cook Inlet next week.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: Here’s a reality check on Trump’s AI pledge
item.description: Trump’s promise to protect power customers’ wallets from data centers covers only part of the costs of expanding AI.

media:content.media:title: A QTS data center is seen in Ashburn, Virginia, on Nov. 20, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman and Peter Behr
item.dc:contributor: By Zack Colman and Peter Behr

item.title: Underdog Democrats lean into data center concerns
item.description: Democrats in particular have seized on the prospect of rising energy costs in the fight for control of Washington and state capitals.

media:content.media:title: Abdul El-Sayed, a Democratic candidate for Senate in Michigan, released a "Terms of Engagement" on data centers.

item.dc:creator: By Timothy Cama, Amelia Davidson and Natalie Fertig
item.dc:contributor: By Timothy Cama, Amelia Davidson and Natalie Fertig

item.title: Trump sidelines climate contrarians in science rollback
item.description: The president has mocked global warming as a “hoax,” but his administration avoided testing that claim in court as it targeted the endangerment finding.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump said Thursday that EPA has rolled back its authority to regulate greenhouse gases.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Trump's big climate reversal sets up a courtroom clash
item.description: EPA has chucked the long-standing finding that greenhouse gas emissions drive climate change, drawing promises of lawsuits from environmentalists and Democrats.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump has undone the greenhouse gas endangerment finding, the foundation for EPA's biggest climate change regulations, as he seeks to boost fossil fuels.

item.dc:creator: By Alex Guillén

item.title: EPA repeals landmark finding that climate pollution is harmful
item.description: The move discards a scientific determination that empowered the government to limit climate pollution.

media:content.media:title: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin testifies last May before a subcommittee of the House Energy and Commerce Committee.

item.dc:creator: By Jean Chemnick

item.title: Schumer dials up oversight pressure on Trump’s Venezuela oil fund
item.description: The Senate minority leader is introducing a bill to direct a GAO investigation of the Trump administration’s handling of Venezuelan oil proceeds.

media:content.media:title: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer has criticized the Trump administration's unilateral moves in Venezuela.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales
